_meta.note: 12 event cards. One fires per world phase, weighted by scenario, drawn without replacement — and each one is a DECISION: two options with forked effects. Option 'a' asserts, option 'b' accommodates; an unanswered event resolves to 'b' at year end (drift is a choice too). DSL documented in docs/model-notes.md; new keys: ap (move delta this year), legalOpening (turns the courts have a live case).
_meta.provenance: Real instruments (REDATA, ILO-169) get a one-line gloss; the events themselves are fictional.

events.name: Tariff threat
events.copy: Washington threatens tariffs on countries that put conditions on tech investment. That includes you.
events.question: Hold the line?

events.name: Chip export crackdown
events.copy: The US tightens the rules on who can buy advanced chips. Every ally that depends on American computing power gets nervous.
events.question: How do you respond?

a.label: Coordinate a joint response with allies

events.name: The data centre offer
events.copy: A tech giant wants to build a huge data centre on your grid, and it wants an answer this year. (Brazil pioneered a law for exactly this moment: REDATA.)
events.question: On whose terms?

a.label: Demand terms — jobs, energy, local benefit

b.label: Wave it through, take the investment

events.name: Big Tech vs your courts
events.copy: Your courts take up a case against a major AI company for training its models on local content without paying.
events.question: Let it run?

events.name: A summit that works
events.copy: A global AI summit actually goes well for once, and the follow-up is yours to host — if you spend the year on it.
events.question: Host it?

events.name: China's tech bank
events.copy: China proposes a BRICS technology bank: cheap computing power, friendly financing, familiar faces. Some of your allies are listening.
events.question: Do you take the meeting?

events.name: America's compute pact
events.copy: The US floats an all-Americas computing pact: cheap access for countries that play by its rules. Your US-leaning allies are tempted.
events.question: Do you take the meeting?

events.name: Minerals boom
events.copy: Mineral prices jump worldwide as AI hardware demand bites. Everyone suddenly remembers what is under your ground.
events.question: Squeeze back?

events.name: The consultation halt
events.copy: A court pauses a big data centre because Indigenous and local communities were never consulted — in much of the world (Brazil included, via ILO-169) that is a real legal duty.
events.question: Consult, or push?

events.name: The water crisis
events.copy: A data centre's cooling system drains a town's reservoir in the middle of a drought. It is on the news every night.
events.question: Own it, or defend it?

a.label: Publish the water data, renegotiate the deal

events.name: The audit
events.copy: An independent audit asks a simple question: where did the promised local benefits go?
events.question: Open the books?

events.name: AI takes a leap
events.copy: AI capability jumps overnight, and nobody outside the superpowers saw it coming.
events.question: Scramble, or absorb?
